qa_pairs.question: When bind-mounting the `/home` filesystem into an Apptainer container, why is it recommended to use an alternate destination directory like `/cluster_home` (e.g., `-B /home:/cluster_home`)?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to use an alternate destination directory like `/cluster_home` when bind-mounting the `/home` filesystem into an Apptainer container to prevent configuration files and programs from your host home directory (such as executables in `$HOME/bin` or Python packages in `$HOME/.local/lib/python3.x`) from interfering with or being used instead of the corresponding software within your container.

qa_pairs.question: What potential issues can arise if you directly bind-mount the `/home` filesystem into an Apptainer container without specifying an alternate destination directory?
qa_pairs.answer: If you directly bind-mount the `/home` filesystem into an Apptainer container without an alternate destination, it can lead to conflicts where configuration files and programs from your host's home directory—like those in `$HOME/bin` or Python packages in `$HOME/.local/lib/python3.x`—might be utilized instead of the software intended to run from within your container.
